zh.lifeSummary: 當John de Washborne於大約1310年出生在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾斯坦福 奧 姆時，他的父親Roger de Wasseborne Coroner Lord of Washbourne and of Stanford36歲，母親Margarett32歲。他於1328年與Isabell在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾伍斯特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1358年在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾奧維波利享年49歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Washborne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Washburn
meanings: English: habitational name from Great and Little Washbourne (Gloucestershire), or Washbourne in Halwell (Devon). The Gloucestershire placename derives from Old English wæsse (genitive wæssan) ‘wet place, swamp, marsh’ + burna ‘spring, stream’. The Devon placename derives from Old English wæsce ‘place for washing’ + burna.
History: John Washburn came to MA in 1626 and settled in Duxbury, MA, in 1632.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When John de Washborne was born about 1310, in Stanford on Teme, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Roger de Wasseborne Coroner Lord of Washbourne and of Stanford, was 36 and his mother, Margarett, was 32. He married Isabell in 1328, in Worcester, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1358, in Overbury, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 49.

Il a épousé Isabell en 1328, à Worcester, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. ja.lifeSummary: John de Washborneが約1310年にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアスタンフォード オン テメで生まれた時，父Roger de Wasseborne Coroner Lord of Washbourne and of Stanfordは36歳，母Margarettは32歳であった。1328年にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアウオルセスターでIsabellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1358年にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアオバーブリーで49歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John de Washborne(이)가 대략 1310년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 스탠퍼드  테에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Roger de Wasseborne Coroner Lord of Washbourne and of Stanford(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Margarett(은)는 32세였다. 그는 1328년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 워스스터에서 Isabell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1358년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 버버이에서 49세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John de Washborne родился около 1310 г. в местности станфорд он тем, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Roger de Wasseborne Coroner Lord of Washbourne and of Stanford было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Margarett, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabell в 1328 г. в местности ворсестир, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1358 г. в местности овирбури, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 49 лет.
